title: Plans for Spring Break
html:
TL;DR

  • Spring 2023 OSU term-break self-brief — Yahir's "fuck off, get to the point" intention list mixing media consumption, household admin, opinion-formation, and physical training
  • Reading/listening queue: finish The 4 Hour Workweek (struck through), the Sam Altman x Lex Fridman podcast episode, Balaji x Pomp video (struck through), begin Marcus Aurelius's Meditations — first vault evidence Yahir is entering the Stoic canon
  • Three explicit opinion-formation tasks: (1) where he stands on AI open-source / OpenAI, (2) his relationship with alcohol, (3) living-situation choice — proto-piercing prompts practice
  • Action items: file taxes with mom, sign up for a therapist (first explicit therapy commitment in the vault), Toastmasters meetings (decide if it's actually worth it), train for a half marathon, get the workout machine to Corvallis, pickleball with Nic, "figure out if maya situation is legit" — first vault appearance of Maya in a pre-relationship-decision frame
  • Closing manifesto: "focus on the small… the time is always now" — same time is always now phrase that recurs in 2024–2025 raws

Notes

A short but high-signal artifact. This is Yahir using a Google Doc as a letter to self checkpoint at the start of OSU spring break 2023, the term that lands him in the "vision for big life" reflective state nine months later.

Three things to flag:

  1. First vault evidence Yahir is reading Marcus Aurelius — the Stoic frame becomes load-bearing in 2024–2025; it starts here.
  2. First explicit therapy commitment — "sign up for a therapist". Worth tracking whether this happens.
  3. Maya status check — "figure out if maya situation is legit" is the earliest decision-mode framing of the maya relationship. Pairs with 2022 07 18 why mom maya jann brenda dad and the later gray hairs/Paige arc.

The half-marathon/workout-machine/pickleball cluster is the body as discipline thread (compare 2026 04 26 pushing the limits of health choose someting have something) showing up two years before the Austin-era version.
